summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard recording of a September 1972 show that dazzles with a monster “Other One” and a flawless second‑set jam marathon.
review: The tape delivers an exceptionally clean SBD capture (aside from a few early glitches on “Loser”) that lets the band’s 1972 peak shine through.  Highlights include a soaring “He’s Gone → The Other One → Me & Bobby McGee” suite, a tight “Greatest Story Ever Told”, and a rapturous second‑set flow that keeps the energy at full throttle from China Cat > Rider to Wharf Rat.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, almost all 4‑5 stars, uniformly praising the musical performance and noting excellent soundboard clarity; only minor recording hiccups are mentioned.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall crisp, vibrant mix with great instrument separation. Early portions suffer from low‑quality SBD artifacts on “Loser” and a missing “Truckin'” segment, but the rest of the set is clean and dynamic.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with tight renditions of Half Step, Big River and a killer China Cat > Rider; the second set erupts into an uninterrupted jam arc (He’s Gone → The Other One → Me & Bobby McGee → The Other One) that showcases the band’s improvisational chemistry.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His wah‑wah work is repeatedly praised, especially on the “Other One” and during the “Greatest Story” solo; tone described as “walking and talking”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lauded for his bass ‘bombs’, jazzy interplay with Bill, and overall fire – many reviewers say “when Phil is on, the band is on”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Consistently noted to be high in the mix with superb rhythm work; his guitar tone on “Me & Bobby McGee” and other cuts receives special praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as “excellent”, “perfect”, and a driving force throughout the long jams.
